metadata.title: Content and Format of Chemistry, Manufacturing and Controls Information and Establishment Description Information for a Vaccine or Related Product

rules.rule_text: The quality and purity of the drug substance cannot be assured solely by downstream testing; proper control of the manufacturing and synthetic process is required. Minimal impurity levels depend on: appropriate quality and purity of starting materials (including seed organisms and reagents); establishment and use of in-process controls; consistent adherence to validated process procedures; and adequacy of final release control testing.
rules.source_section: Part 1 — CMC Section, I. Drug Substance (preamble)
rules.verbatim_quote: The quality and purity of the drug substance cannot be assured solely by downstream testing, but depends on proper control of the manufacturing and synthetic process as well. Proper control and attainment of minimal levels of impurities depends on: appropriate quality and purity of the starting materials, including the seed organisms, and reagents; establishment and use of in-process controls for intermediates; consistent adherence to validated process procedures; and adequacy of the final (release) control testing of the drug substance.

rules.rule_text: The drug substance description section must contain the biological name (including strain and/or clone designation) or chemical name (including any established USAN name), source of the cells (including microbes), active components of cell fractions or purified antigens, physical and chemical properties of the synthetic drug substance, any chemical modification or conjugation in detail, and a list of inactive substances present.
rules.verbatim_quote: The biological name (including strain and/or clone designation) or chemical name, including any established USAN name, should be provided. The description should also include the source of the cells, including microbes, from which the drug substances were derived, the active components of the cell fractions or purified antigens, and the physical and chemical properties of the synthetic drug substance. Any chemical modification or conjugation of the drug substance should be described in detail. Also, a list of any inactive substances, which may be present in the drug substance, should be provided.

rules.rule_text: Characterization must include analytical testing for identity, purity, potency, and stability. Test results must include actual data (tabular data, chromatograms/spectra, gels/immunoblots, cytometric histograms). Results for quantitative assays must be presented as actual data, not as 'Pass' or 'Fail.'
rules.verbatim_quote: This section should contain a description of all analytical testing performed to characterize the drug substance with respect to identity, purity, potency, and stability... Results for quantitative assays should be presented as actual data, not generally as 'Pass' or 'Fail.'
rules.exceptions: Some tests may not be applicable to all substances.

rules.rule_text: Biological activity characterization must include results of all relevant in vivo and in vitro biological testing (bioassays) on the manufacturer's reference standard lot. For each bioassay, the application must include: protocol description, control standards used, validation of inherent variability, and established acceptance limits. Characteristics of specific antibodies used in immunochemical or serological assays must also be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: A description and results of all relevant in vivo and in vitro biological testing (bioassays) performed on the manufacturer's reference standard lot or other relevant lots to demonstrate the potency and activity(ies) of the drug substance should be provided. This section should include a complete description of the protocol used for each bioassay, the control standards used, the validation of the inherent variability of the test, and the established acceptance limits for each assay.

rules.rule_text: The application must identify every manufacturer responsible for any portion of manufacture or testing of the drug substance, including name(s), address(es), FDA registration number, pertinent organizational information, operations performed, and responsibilities delegated. Independent contractors, subsidiaries, and other locations must be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: The application should include the name(s), address(es), FDA registration number, and other pertinent organizational information for each manufacturer responsible for any portion of the manufacture or testing operations for the drug substance. This may include independent contractors or other company subsidiaries serving as contractors, or other locations/sites owned and operated by the applicant.

rules.rule_text: A simple floor diagram of the general facility layout must be provided for each manufacturing location, tracing the drug substance through the manufacturing process. The diagram must clearly depict the relationship of each manufacturing area/suite/room, identify adjacent areas (including non-subject areas), and enable visualization of production flow and identification of contamination risks.
rules.verbatim_quote: For each manufacturing location, a simple floor diagram of the general layout of the facilities, which traces the drug substance through the manufacturing process should be included... The diagrams should be sufficiently clear to enable visualization of the production flow and to identify adjacent operations that may create particular concerns, e.g., the proximity of live viral cultures to inactivated intermediates or final products, segregation of animal facilities, etc.

rules.rule_text: A comprehensive list of all additional products manufactured or manipulated in the same areas used to produce the drug substance must be provided, including type and developmental status of those products, areas where introduced, manufacturing steps in multi-use areas, and whether shared product-contact equipment is used (and if so, cleaning/validation details).
rules.source_section: Part 1, I.B.3 — Manufacture of Other Products
rules.verbatim_quote: A comprehensive list of all additional products that are manufactured or manipulated in the same areas used to produce the drug substance that is the subject of this application should be provided... the applicant should indicate whether the production of other products will utilize the same product contact equipment and, if so, how that equipment will be cleaned and validated between operations.

rules.rule_text: For all manufacturing areas (including cell bank preparation and animal handling areas), contamination precaution information must be provided: air quality classification validated during operations; procedures/facility design for contamination and cross-contamination control; general equipment design (open/closed system, sterile/non-sterile); and in-process controls to prevent/identify contamination or cross-contamination.
rules.verbatim_quote: For all areas in which operations for the preparation of cell banks and product manufacturing are performed, including areas for the handling of animals used in production, the following information concerning contamination precautions taken to prevent contamination or cross-contamination, should be provided: air quality classification of a room or area in which an operation is performed, as validated and measured during operations; a brief, narrative description of the procedures and/or facility design features for the control of contamination, cross contamination, and containment... a description of the in-process controls performed to prevent or to identify contamination or cross contamination.

rules.rule_text: A list of all raw materials (culture media, buffers, resins, chemicals, columns, etc.) used in manufacture of the drug substance, with their tests and specifications or reference to official compendia, must be provided. For custom reagents (monoclonal antibodies, enzymes, uncommon amino acids, glycolipids), the vendor/supplier, specificity, origin, and manufacturing scheme must be described in detail.
rules.verbatim_quote: A list of all materials (culture media, buffers, resins for peptide synthesis, chemicals, columns, etc.) used in the manufacture of the drug substance, and their tests and specifications, or reference to official compendia, should be provided... Custom reagents, such as monoclonal antibodies, enzymes, other proteins, uncommon amino acids and derivatives, or glycolipids, used in purification or production of the drug substance, should be described in detail, including identification of the vendor/supplier, specificity, and origin, including the manufacturing scheme, if applicable.
rules.exceptions: Representative certificates of analysis from the supplier may substitute for redundant testing at the purchasing manufacturer if testing methods at the vendor are approved.

rules.rule_text: A complete visual flow chart of the manufacturing process must be provided for each drug substance, showing: production steps, equipment and materials used, room/area where each operation is performed, complete list of in-process controls and tests at each step, and in-process holding steps with time and temperature limits.
rules.verbatim_quote: In this section, a complete visual representation of the manufacturing process flow should be provided for each drug substance... This flow chart should show the steps in production, equipment and materials used, room or area where the operation is performed (may reference diagrams in other sections of the application), and a complete list of the in-process controls and tests performed on the product at each step. In-process holding steps should be included, with time and temperature limits indicated.

rules.rule_text: For vaccines using animal sources (including fertilized avian eggs), detailed information must be provided including: species and age of animals; health status (e.g., specific pathogen free); results of adventitious agent screening; animal husbandry practices (quarantine procedures); veterinary and laboratory monitoring; inoculation description; and tissues harvested and harvest method.
rules.verbatim_quote: Detailed information on any animals used for the propagation of microorganisms, or production of recombinant proteins, for use as vaccines should include, but is not limited to: the species and age of the animals; the health status of the animals, e.g., specific pathogen free; the results of adventitious agent screening; the animal husbandry practices, e.g., quarantine procedures, used to ensure the suitability of the animals; the veterinary and laboratory monitoring used to ensure the suitability of the animals; a description of the inoculation of the animals; and a description of the tissues harvested and the method of harvest.

rules.rule_text: For virus sources used in vaccine production, a detailed description of the virus seed must include: original source of the virus; passage history of the virus strains; details of the seed lot system; and culture techniques for virus seed maintenance.
rules.verbatim_quote: This section should include a detailed description of the virus seed used for vaccine production. The information submitted should include, but is not limited to: the original source of the virus; the passage history of the virus strains; details of the seed lot system; the culture techniques for virus seed maintenance.

rules.rule_text: For animal cells used in vaccine production, measures taken to remove, inactivate, or prevent contamination of the product from any adventitious agent present in the cell substrate must be described. For metazoan cells specifically, results of tests for bioburden (bacteria and fungi) and mycoplasma must be submitted for both MCB and WCB; virus testing must be submitted for metazoan cell substrates.
rules.source_section: Part 1, I.C.3.c.ii — Animal Cells; I.C.3.c.iv Characterization and Testing
rules.verbatim_quote: Cells of animal origin may harbor adventitious agents and consequently pose a potentially greater risk to humans if not properly controlled... The measures taken to remove, inactivate, or prevent contamination of the product from any adventitious agent present in the cell substrate should be described... For metazoan cells, results of tests for the presence of bioburden (bacteria and fungi) and mycoplasma should be submitted for the MCB and WCB. The results of virus testing of metazoan cell substrates to detect possible contaminating viruses, using appropriate screening tests designed to detect a wide spectrum of viruses and relevant specific tests based on the cultivation history of the cell line, should be submitted.
cross_references: ICH Q5A (Viral Safety Evaluation of Biotechnology Products, 11/29/95)

rules.rule_text: For recombinant DNA (rDNA) derived products and rDNA-modified cell substrates, detailed information must be provided on the host cells and the source/function of component parts of the recombinant gene construct, including: host cell source/phenotype/genotype; gene construct with complete nucleotide sequence of the coding region and regulatory elements; restriction enzyme digestion maps; and vector description including origins of replication, antibiotic resistance genes, promoters, and enhancers.
rules.source_section: Part 1, I.C.3.c.iii — Genetic Constructs and Recombinant Cell Lines
rules.verbatim_quote: For recombinant DNA (rDNA) derived products and rDNA-modified cell substrates, detailed information should be provided regarding the host cells, and the source and function of the component parts of the recombinant gene construct... The complete nucleotide sequence of the coding region and regulatory elements of the expression construct, with translated amino acid sequence, should be provided, including annotation designating all important sequence features.

rules.rule_text: A description of cell banking procedures must be provided, including: the banking system used; size of cell banks; container and closure system; methods, reagents, and media for preparation; cryopreservation and storage conditions; in-process controls; and storage conditions. Procedures to avoid microbial contamination and cross-contamination and to trace banked cells must also be described. Cell banks must be stored to prevent catastrophic loss (e.g., multiple freezers or different sites).
rules.verbatim_quote: A description of the cell banking procedures used should be provided, including: the banking system used; the size of the cell banks; the container and closure system used; a detailed description of the methods, reagents and media used for preparation of the cell banks; the conditions employed for cryopreservation and storage; in-process controls; and storage conditions... The cell bank system generally consists of two tiers: a Master Cell Bank (MCB), and a Working Cell Bank (WCB) generated from the MCB for vaccine manufacturing.
rules.exceptions: A third tier 'Primary Cell Bank' may be established for primary cells.

rules.rule_text: The Master Cell Bank (MCB) must be identified with a complete history and characterization including: biological/chemical derivation method; biochemistry (cell surface markers, isoenzyme analysis); specific identifying characteristics (morphology, serotype); karyology and tumorigenicity; virulence markers; genetic markers; purity of culture; and media and components (e.g., serum).
rules.verbatim_quote: The cells comprising the MCB should be identified and a complete history and characterization of the MCB should be provided, including, as appropriate for the given cells: the biological or chemical method used to derive the cell bank; biochemistry (cell surface markers, isoenzyme analysis, specific protein or mRNA, etc.); specific identifying characteristics (morphology, serotype, etc.); karyology and tumorigenicity; virulence markers; genetic markers; purity of culture; and media and components (e.g., serum).

rules.rule_text: For rDNA derived drug substances, End-of-Production Cells (EPC) characterization must be provided to demonstrate that the biological production system is consistent during growth. This must include: results of phenotypic/genotypic marker analysis to confirm identity and purity; results of testing supporting freedom from adventitious agent contamination; and results of restriction enzyme analysis of the gene constructs in the EPC.
rules.source_section: Part 1, I.C.3.c.iv — End of Production Cells (EPC)
rules.verbatim_quote: For r-DNA derived drug substances, a detailed description of the characterization of the EPC that demonstrates that the biological production system is consistent during growth should be provided. The results of the analysis of the EPC for phenotypic or genotypic markers to confirm identity and purity should be included. This section should also contain the results of testing supporting the freedom of the EPC from contamination by adventitious agents.

rules.rule_text: A completed (executed) representative batch record of the process of production of the drug substance must be provided in the CMC section.
rules.verbatim_quote: A completed (executed) representative batch record of the process of production of the drug substance should be provided.

rules.rule_text: For all in-process testing indicated in the flow charts, a brief description of sampling procedures and test methods must be provided. For testing performed at significant phases of production, the criteria for accepting or rejecting an in-process batch must be specified.
rules.verbatim_quote: For all in-process testing indicated in the Flow Charts, a brief description of the sampling procedures and the test methods used should be provided. For testing performed at significant phases of production, the criteria for accepting or rejecting an in-process batch should be specified.

rules.rule_text: Process validation summary reports, including protocols and results, must be provided for each critical process or factor that affects drug substance specifications (i.e., a decision to accept or reject a batch). Validation study reports must document the variability in each process as it relates to final specifications and quality, with statistical rigor.
rules.verbatim_quote: A summary report, including protocols and results, should be provided for the validation studies of each critical process or factor that affects drug substance specifications, i.e., a decision to accept or reject a batch... The validation study reports with statistical rigor should document the variability in each process as it relates to final specifications and quality.
cross_references: Guideline on General Principles of Process Validation, 1987

rules.rule_text: For inactivation processes, inactivation or killing curves (or tabular representation based on historical performance) must be provided. Validation of the titration method to measure residual live agents, including sensitivity in a background of inactivated agents, must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: Inactivation or killing curves, or a tabular representation, based on historical performance should be provided. Validation of the titration method to measure residual live agents, including sensitivity in a background of inactivated agents, should be provided.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing consistency must be demonstrated by manufacturing at least three, preferably consecutive, batches of drug substance. Reference standards must be used in assuring consistency of product characteristics.
rules.verbatim_quote: Consistency of the manufacturing process for each vaccine component should be demonstrated by manufacturing at least three, preferably consecutive, batches of drug substance. The establishment and use of reference standards in assuring consistency in product characteristics should be described.

rules.rule_text: Drug substance specifications must include assays for identity, purity, potency (biologic effect), physicochemical measurements that predict potency, and stability measures. Potency should, where appropriate, be presented relative to the respective U.S. Reference Standard as defined in 21 CFR 610.20. Validation results for each specification, including estimates of variability and upper/lower limits, must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: This section should contain the specifications and tests for each drug substance. These should include assays for identity, purity, potency (biologic effect), physicochemical measurements which predict potency, and where applicable, measures of stability... Where appropriate, potency should be presented relative to the respective U.S. Reference Standard as defined in 21 CFR 610.20.

rules.rule_text: An impurities profile for the drug substance must be submitted. The identity and quantity of impurities must be provided along with supporting analytical data. Impurities requiring characterization and quantitation include: product-related impurities (variants or alterations of antigen during processing or storage); and process-related impurities (media components, cell substrate proteins or nucleic acids, and process reagents not removed by purification).
rules.verbatim_quote: This section should include a discussion of the impurities in the drug substance. The identity and quantity of impurities should be provided along with the analytical data (gels, elution profiles, Western blots, etc.) which support the impurities profile. Impurities that should be characterized and quantitated include: product related impurities (variants or alterations of antigen occurring during processing or storage); Process related impurities - media components; - cell substrate proteins or nucleic acids; or - process reagents which have not been removed by the purification process.

rules.rule_text: For any reprocessing performed on a drug substance, the application must include: conditions/criteria indicating the need for reprocessing; description of the reprocessing step; the SOP for the step; description of any modified in-process controls; modified batch number documentation in Batch Production Record; and evidence from validation studies that identity, purity, potency, and stability are preserved for reprocessed batches.
rules.verbatim_quote: This section should include detailed information on any reprocessing that may be done on each drug substance. The information provided for each reprocessing procedure should include: a description of the conditions or criteria... a description of the reprocessing step; the Standard Operating Procedure for the step; a description of any additional or modified in-process controls or specifications... the evidence derived from validation studies which assures that product identity, purity, potency, and stability is preserved for re-processed batches.

rules.rule_text: A description of the drug substance container and closure system, including compatibility with the drug substance, must be submitted. Supplier name, address, and results of compatibility, toxicity, and biological tests must be included. For sterile drug substances, evidence of container and closure integrity for the duration of the proposed expiry period must be provided.
rules.source_section: Part 1, I.H — Container and Closure System
rules.verbatim_quote: A description of the container and closure system, and its compatibility with the drug substance should be submitted. The submission should include detailed information concerning the supplier, address, and the results of compatibility, toxicity and biological tests. If the drug substance is intended to be sterile, evidence of container and closure integrity for the duration of the proposed expiry period should be provided.
rules.exceptions: A Drug Master File (DMF) may be referenced.

rules.rule_text: Drug substance stability information must be provided at each holding step, including any in-process material, in accordance with stability testing guidelines for biological products.
rules.verbatim_quote: This section should contain information on the stability of the drug substance and any in-process material at each holding step, as outlined in 'Stability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products, 10/27/93,' 'Quality of Biotechnological Products: Stability Testing of Biotechnological/Biological Products, 11/30/95' and 'Guideline for Submitting Documentation for the Stability of Human Drugs and Biologics, 1987.'
cross_references: ICH Q5C (Stability Testing of Biological/Biotechnological Products)

rules.rule_text: The drug product section must contain information on all drug substances and excipients in the final product. For all ingredients of human or animal origin, testing results or certificates of analysis demonstrating freedom from adventitious agents must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: For all ingredients of human or animal origin, testing results or certificates of analysis demonstrating their freedom from adventitious agents should be provided.

rules.rule_text: A list of all components in the drug product, including drug substance(s) and other ingredients, with their unit doses and batch quantities specified, must be provided. For adjuvants, the chemical formula and precise quantity per unit dose must be stated, with the method (assay or calculation) indicated.
rules.source_section: Part 1, II.A.1 — Drug Product Composition; II.A.1.c Adjuvant
rules.verbatim_quote: A list should be provided of all components in the drug product, including drug substance(s) and other ingredients, with their unit doses and batch quantities specified... This section should contain a list of the chemical formula and precise quantity of each adjuvant per unit dose. Whether the quantity of adjuvant is determined by assay or by calculation should be indicated and the method used should be described.
rules.exceptions: For some inactive ingredients, the quantity may be expressed as percent or molarity.

rules.rule_text: Each preservative must be identified by chemical name as well as any trade name or reference to compendial sources. A rationale must be provided for inclusion of a preservative in single-dose drug products. Results of preservative effectiveness studies must be included or referenced.
rules.verbatim_quote: Each preservative should be identified by chemical as well as any trade name or reference to compendial sources. A rationale should be provided for the inclusion of a preservative in single dose drug products. The results of the preservative effectiveness studies should be included or reference may be made to other files.

rules.rule_text: Drug product specifications and methods must describe tests to assure identity, purity, strength and/or potency, and lot-to-lot consistency of the finished product. Certificates of analysis and analytical results for at least three consecutive batches must be provided.
rules.source_section: Part 1, II.D.2 — Drug Product Specifications and Methods
rules.verbatim_quote: A description of all test methods selected to assure the identity, purity, strength and/or potency, as well as the lot-to-lot consistency of the finished product and the specifications used for the drug product should be submitted. Certificates of analysis and analytical results for at least three consecutive batches should be provided.

rules.rule_text: A Master Production Record (MPR) for the drug product must be provided, including complete manufacturing instructions for adsorption (if applicable), formulation, filling, labeling, and packaging. Results of studies validating compatibility of components including adjuvants/preservatives must be provided. Lot-to-lot consistency of the drug product must be demonstrated.
rules.verbatim_quote: A Master Production Record (MPR) for the drug product should be provided, including complete manufacturing instructions for adsorption (if applicable), formulation, filling, labeling, and packaging... Results of studies validating the compatibility of the components including the adjuvants and/or preservatives, if applicable, should be provided. Lot-to-lot consistency of the drug product should be demonstrated.

rules.rule_text: For lyophilized drug products, a validation summary for lyophilization must be provided, including: narrative description of validation (or protocol); certification that IQ and OQ have been completed; validation data summary; explanation of all excursions or failures; and deviation reports and results of investigations of all excursions or failures.
rules.verbatim_quote: A validation summary for lyophilization of the drug product should be given which includes: A narrative description of the validation (or protocol); Certification that IQ and OQ have been completed; A validation data summary; Explanation of all excursions or failures; and Deviation reports and results of investigations of all excursions or failures.

rules.rule_text: Drug product stability section must state the proposed expiration dating period and recommended storage conditions. A stability study protocol must be provided including testing for: potency; physicochemical measurements which are potency-indicating; moisture (if lyophilized); pH (if appropriate); sterility or control of bioburden; viability of cells (if frozen/thawed); pyrogenicity; and general safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: This section should state the proposed expiration dating period for the drug product and the recommended storage conditions... A stability study protocol should be provided which includes, but is not limited to, testing for: potency; physicochemical measurements which are potency-indicating; moisture, if lyophilized; pH, if appropriate; sterility or control of bioburden; viability of cells, if frozen and thawed; pyrogenicity; and general safety.

rules.rule_text: If the investigational drug formulation differs from the to-be-marketed finished product (formulation, manufacturing process, or site), a discussion of these differences must be submitted. If formulations differ, data to support comparability, bioequivalence, and/or pharmacokinetic equivalence must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: A discussion of any differences in formulation, manufacturing process, or site between the clinical trials materials and commercial production batches of drug substance and drug product should be submitted. If there are differences, a complete description of these differences should be included. If an investigational drug formulation was different from that of the to-be-marketed finished product, data to support comparability, bioequivalence and/or pharmacokinetic equivalence of the two formulations should be provided.

rules.rule_text: An environmental assessment (EA), as outlined in 21 CFR Part 25, or a request for categorical exclusion with basis for the exclusion, must be submitted with the CMC section of the license application.
rules.verbatim_quote: An environmental assessment (EA), as outlined in 21 CFR Part 25, or a request for a categorical exclusion with the basis for the exclusion, should be submitted. If an EA is appropriate, it should include a description of the action that is being considered and should address all the components involved in the manufacture and disposal of the product.
rules.exceptions: Categorical exclusion available with basis stated.

rules.rule_text: For all release or acceptance testing performed on drug substance(s) or product, method validation information as described in the 'Guideline for Submitting Samples and Analytical Data for Methods Validation' must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: For all release or acceptance testing performed on the drug substance(s) or product, information as described in the 'Guideline for Submitting Samples and Analytical Data for Methods Validation' should be provided.
cross_references: FDA Guideline for Submitting Samples and Analytical Data for Methods Validation, 1987

rules.rule_text: The Establishment Description section must include a floor diagram for each manufacturing location showing product, personnel, equipment, waste, and air flow; identification of areas served by each air handling unit; and air pressure differentials between adjacent areas. Note: Establishment Description is not required for recombinant DNA derived vaccines or synthetic peptide vaccines.
rules.source_section: Part 2 — Establishment Description Section, II. General Information
rules.verbatim_quote: For each manufacturing location, a floor diagram should be included that indicates the general facility layout. The following information should be provided on each floor diagram and/or in an accompanying narrative: Product, personnel, equipment, waste and air flow; An illustration or indication of which areas are served by each air handling unit; and Air pressure differentials between adjacent areas... The information contained in this section need not be submitted for recombinant DNA derived vaccines or synthetic peptide vaccines.
rules.exceptions: Not required for recombinant DNA derived vaccines or synthetic peptide vaccines.

rules.rule_text: Water purification systems used in manufacturing must be described and validated. A validation summary must include: narrative of validation process with acceptance criteria; certification that IQ and OQ have been completed; length of validation period; parameters monitored and tests performed; monitoring frequency at each point of use; validation data summary; and explanation of all excursions or failures with deviation reports.
rules.verbatim_quote: A validation summary should be provided containing: a narrative description of the validation process (or protocol) including acceptance criteria; certification that installation qualification (IQ) and operational qualification (OQ) have been completed; the length of the validation period; the parameters monitored and tests performed; the frequency of monitoring each point of use during the validation period; a validation data summary; and an explanation of all excursions or failures, including deviation reports and results of investigations.

rules.rule_text: Methods to prevent contamination and cross-contamination must be documented in the Establishment Description section, including cleaning validation for both dedicated and shared equipment. For shared equipment: cleaning procedures/reagents with effectiveness rationale; and a validation report addressing sampling/analytical methods, sensitivities, and specificities must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: The following information regarding methods to prevent contamination and cross contamination should be provided... For shared equipment: a brief description of the cleaning procedures and cleaning reagents; a rationale for the cleaning procedures chosen which addresses their effectiveness for the residual products to be removed; and a validation report describing the cleaning validation procedures for removal of product residues and cleaning agents. The report should identify the sampling and analytical methods used and address their sensitivities and specificities.
